name: Okinawa Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the bright future of the prefecture
the warmth of Okinawa's southern climate, the surrounding ocean, and the energy of its people

description: A white field charged at the centre with a red emblem made up of three concentric ring-segments forming a stylised letter 'O': a broad outer red ring, a white middle ring, and a small red disc at the centre.
history: Okinawa's prefectural symbol was adopted on 25 October 1972, five months after Okinawa reverted from American to Japanese administration on 15 May 1972. The flag, with the symbol on a white field, has served as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: three concentric ring-segments stylising the Roman letter 'O' for Okinawa: an outer red ring representing the surrounding ocean and the prefecture's reach to the world, a middle white ring representing the prefecture's development, and a central red disc representing the prefecture itself.
